message:
fix(http): tri-state Limited body distinguishes clean EOF from failure

Limited<B> previously used a single bool (limit_exceeded) to track
terminal state, which meant clean EOF (None) would be followed by
another poll of the inner body on repoll. Now uses a LimitedState enum:

- Open: normal operation, polls inner body
- Completed: clean EOF reached; repoll returns None idempotently
- Failed: limit exceeded or inner error; repoll returns
  PolledAfterCompletion

This prevents re-polling a completed inner body (which may panic or
produce undefined behavior) while maintaining fail-closed semantics
for error terminals. is_end_stream() and size_hint() also respect
the terminal state.

New tests:
- limited_body_fail_closes_after_terminal_inner_error
- limited_body_does_not_repoll_completed_inner_body

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
